old_claim.note: Democracy & Republicanism provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: French Revolutionary nationalism joined popular sovereignty and citizenship to national unity, drawing on republican political traditions as a historical foundation rather than a technical dependency.

source_shape.source_locator: Pages 8-9 explain that the nation-state became an attractive model after the French Revolution of 1789-1815 and that its patriotic national unity helped nationalism become a major nineteenth-century political force.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: Cambridge links the French revolutionary nation-state model to rights of citizens, patriotic unity, and popular political legitimacy.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Those ideas establish an intellectual and institutional predecessor relationship, not a technical input consumed by nationalist organization.

ontology_before: democracy_republicanism was modeled as a capability directly supplied to nationalism_modern.
ontology_after: democracy_republicanism is modeled as documented historical context preceding nationalism_modern, not a consumed technical component.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: nationalism_modern->democracy_republicanism is now historical_predecessor rather than enabling.
would_reject_if: Evidence showed that nationalism_modern operationally consumed democracy_republicanism as a technical component rather than drawing on it as historical context.
short_reason: Republican popular sovereignty is historical political lineage rather than a component capability.
